response.justification: Sarah Smith is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a broad polyglot background, but she is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position has a hard requirement for native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is entirely absent from her profile. Beyond the language barrier, the role demands deep C programming for embedded firmware, Linux kernel-level expertise, and security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) — none of which appear in her experience. Her strongest assets are in TypeScript/Node.js web development, not embedded networking or security features.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts demonstrates adaptability and engineering depth
Some exposure to C/C++ at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and in academic projects shows she is not entirely foreign to systems-level programming
Strong culture of engineering discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and knowledge-sharing align with collaborative team expectations
concerns: Completely lacks Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a non-negotiable hard requirement for the role
No demonstrated experience with embedded networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or Linux kernel development and modification
Professional history is overwhelmingly web and cloud-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), with no evidence of LAN switch firmware, chip-vendor SDKs, or embedded device software

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
Broad engineering experience and some C/systems exposure
